behaviors.description: The request sent to the external API always includes the complete list of valid taxonomy labels.
behaviors.justification: This is primarily module-to-client handoff correctness (request builder + API adapter). A unit test of prompt/request formatting could pass while wiring drops or mutates labels. Rejected System because the behavior text does not require validating a real external response contract or round-trip side effect; it only checks outbound payload contents.
behaviors.test_description: Run the classifier through its real request-construction path with a test double API client/server that captures outbound payload, then assert the taxonomy list in the sent request exactly matches configured labels.

behaviors.description: When the API returns a label that is not in the taxonomy, the module returns "unknown" instead of forwarding the unexpected value to the caller.
behaviors.justification: Failure risk is at response parsing/validation handoff across modules, not model quality. A pure unit test of the normalizer may miss integration paths that bypass validation. Rejected Agentic because correctness here does not depend on real model judgment quality; any stubbed invalid label exercises the rule.
behaviors.test_description: Use a stub API response containing an out-of-taxonomy label through the full classifier call path and assert the public module result is exactly "unknown".

behaviors.description: For identical raw API responses, the module's normalization step always produces the same final label.
behaviors.justification: This is deterministic normalization logic at a local function/object boundary with no required I/O or cross-module reality signal.
behaviors.test_description: Call normalization with the same raw response fixture multiple times and assert identical normalized label output each time.

behaviors.description: On repeated live API calls for the same document and taxonomy, the returned label remains consistent unless the provider response itself changes.
behaviors.justification: Behavior explicitly depends on real AI API output behavior across repeated calls (LLM non-determinism / provider variation). Stubs would hide this risk. Rejected Workflow because this is repeated single-step classification, not a multi-agentic pipeline.
behaviors.test_description: Execute repeated real provider classifications for the same input/taxonomy, log raw provider outputs and final labels, and assert label changes occur only when provider response content changes.

behaviors.description: Request construction enforces the configured token budget before dispatch (for example truncate/reject according to policy).
behaviors.justification: This is orchestration between tokenizer/budget policy and dispatch gate. Unit tests of truncation/rejection logic alone can miss that dispatch still occurs (or is blocked incorrectly). Rejected System because behavior text does not require real external contract validation; enforcement is explicitly before dispatch.
behaviors.test_description: Run end-to-end within process using real request builder and dispatch boundary with controlled long inputs; assert policy outcome (truncate/reject) and verify dispatch invocation matches policy.

behaviors.description: Provider-reported token usage for a typical document stays within the configured per-call budget.
behaviors.justification: This requires a real external API round-trip and checking provider-reported usage contract fields. A mock can always fabricate compliant usage and hide real provider accounting/contract mismatches. Rejected Agentic because the assertion is about external usage/billing contract, not classification quality or model decision correctness.
behaviors.test_description: Make real API calls for representative documents and assert returned usage metadata from provider responses is present and <= configured budget for each call.

behaviors.description: When the API signals that the request rate is too high, the module waits and retries rather than returning an error to the caller.
behaviors.justification: This is retry/backoff orchestration across modules (error mapping, scheduler/timer, retry loop). A unit test of retry function alone can miss wiring failures. Rejected System because behavior text does not explicitly require real external error-contract verification; a controlled in-process stub returning rate-limit signals can expose the logic.
behaviors.test_description: Drive classifier with a stubbed provider that returns rate-limit error first, then success; assert delay/backoff path is invoked and final caller-visible result is success rather than immediate error.
